statement.markdown: Given a singly linked list, implement a function to append a new element to the end of the list. The linked list is represented by the Node class with a val and a next pointer. The head of the linked list is given.
statement.inputFormat: Head of the linked list and a new value to append
statement.outputFormat: Head of the modified linked list

hints.text: Consider what happens when the input linked list is empty

editorial.title: Editorial Title
editorial.intuition: The correct approach is to traverse the linked list until we reach the last node, then append the new node.
editorial.bruteForce: Brute force approach is not efficient for this problem. It involves creating a new list, then appending the new node to it, then returning the new list.
editorial.betterSolution: Traverse the linked list until we reach the last node, then append the new node.
editorial.optimalSolution: We can use a simple iterative approach to traverse the linked list and append the new node.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: We can prove the correctness of our approach by considering all possible cases (e.g. empty list, single node list, multiple node list).
commonMistakes: Not considering the case of an empty list
Not properly handling the new node

functionSignature.java: public Node append(Node head, int val);
functionSignature.python: def append(self, head: Node, val: int) -> Node:
